texts.full_text: he German Roughhaired Pointer (Deutsch Stichelhaar) is a versatile hunting dog that originated in Frankfurt, Germany. The breed was developed in the early 1900s and is a cross between German sheepdogs and rough-haired "standing dogs". Appearance The Stichelhaar is a medium-sized dog, weighing around . They are around the withers. The eyes are oval and chestnut colored and the ears are flat against the head. They may have brown, white, mixed or chestnut coloring. Their coat is long and bristly. Personality Stichelhaars are tough dogs. They are balanced, calm, resistant and couStichelhaars are tough dogs. They are balanced, calm, resistant, and courageous. Their calm and balanced

texts.full_text: rathon. Kevin Pates has been a familiar voice on race day for many years, providing commentary and insights as runners make their way through the course. John Leppi was instrumental in the early days of the marathon, helping to establish it as a premier event in the region. The 477th Medical Company has ensured the safety and well-being of participants for over four decades, while Bill and Nancy Olsen, along with Chuck and Sally Hegg, have been steadfast volunteers at their respective aid stations. Grandma's Marathon is more than just a race;

texts.full_text: about this from the ACPI spec here (http://www.acpi.info/spec.htm), specifically sections 5.2.16 and 5.2.17. Basically, you use the SRAT to determine which memory ranges are associated with which CpUs, and you use the SLIT to determine the relative cost of using a particular CPU/memory range. Both of these tables are optional, but in my experience, most NUMA systems at least have a useful SRAT. As far as initialization goes, I don't think I can help much. You might want to look in to how processors are brought up on the Linux kernel

texts.preview_head: with MBSR and CBT participants showing a more rapid recovery compared to the control group. This indicates that both interventions were effective in promoting a more stress response, even on the initi
texts.preview_tail: nd promoting more adaptive stress responses. These interventions may be valuable tools in the management of stress and the promotion of health and well-being. Reference(s): - Study authored by [Author
texts.full_text: with MBSR and CBT participants showing a more rapid recovery compared to the control group. This indicates that both interventions were effective in promoting a more stress response, even on the initial stressor exposure. Additionally, the study found that both MBSR and CBT interventions were effective in reducing self-reported stress and improving overall well-being. Participants in both intervention groups reported reductions in perceived stress and increases in mindfulness and self-compassion, relative to the control group. These findings provide the first evidence that MBSR and CBT interventions can enhance HPA axis habituation and promote more adaptive stress responses. The results suggest that both interventions may have significant benefits for stress-related health outcomes and may be useful for individuals experiencing stress. Future research should aim to replicate and extend these findings to more and clinical populations. Additionally, further investigation is needed to determine the long-term effects of these interventions and to identify the specific mechanisms through which MBSR and CBT promote more adaptive stress responses. In conclusion, the present study demonstrates the efficacy of MBSR and CBT interventions in enhancing cortisol habituation and promoting more adaptive stress responses. These interventions may be valuable tools in the management of stress and the promotion of health and well-being. Reference(s): - Study authored by [Author

texts.full_text: re supported by substantial evidence and that the claimant met the statutory criteria for benefits under the Federal Coal Mine Health and Safety Act of 1969, 30 U.S.C. § 901 et seq. Clinchfield argues that the Board erred in upholding the ALJ’s determination that the claimant’s respiratory impairment is causally related to his coal mine employment. Specifically, Clinchfield challenges the ALJ’s reliance on chest X-ray evidence and the credibility of the medical testimony presented. The government and the claimant respond that the Board’s decision is entitled to deference under the Administrative Procedure Act and that the ALJ properly applied the statutory presumption of total disability under 20 C.F.R. § 727.203. We review the Board’s decision under the arbitrary and capricious standard, deferring to the Board’s factual findings if supported by substantial evidence in the record. , 502 U.S. 520, 525 (1992). Under the Black Lung Benefits Act, a claimant is entitled to benefits if he proves: (1) he has a totally disabling respiratory disease; (2) arising out of coal mine employment. 30 U.S.C. § 923. If the claimant establishes pneumoconiosis through a qualifying chest X-ray, a rebuttable presumption of total disability arises. 20 C.F.R. § 727.203. Here,
